rows.content: PAGE: COLS+ - The number of columns that are printable via the
destination printer.  In most cases, this is 80.
LINES+- The number of printed lines per page. Normally this value
is 66 for most line printers. Certain laser printers (HP LaserJet
series) reserve space at the top and bottom of the page which
reduces the number of printable lines.  In the case of the LaserJet,
MARGINS: TOP   +- Specifies the number of blank lines to print
before the body and/or header line is printed.
BOTTOM+- Specifies the number of blank lines to print
after the body text and/or footer is printed.
LEFT  +- The number of columns to reserve on the left edge of
the page before and text is printed.
RIGHT +- The number of columns to format the header and
footer lines to, relative to the page columns defined earlier.
